name: TMA reporting period and statistics pie
description: Refine TMA reporting periods so Home, household summaries, statistics, and expense-list date filters share the requested current/previous month, week, year, and custom date range semantics while Statistics renders a selected-period pie chart with percentage legends.

planned: Use shared TMA period helpers for `Tháng này`, `Tháng trước`, `Tuần này`, `Tuần trước`, `Năm nay`, `Năm ngoái`, and custom date-from/date-to ranges.
Keep the reporting period default as current month.
Align expense-list date filter presets with shared reporting period semantics while preserving sort, household, and category filters.
Rebuild TMA Statistics around the selected reporting period with pie/donut category breakdown and legend percentages.
Keep budget periods monthly and avoid weekly/yearly budget semantics.
verification: GitNexus impact checks for `createCurrentMonthPeriodSelection`, `PeriodPickerPage`, `FinanceSummaryCard`, and the indexed Statistics/ExpenseFilter route wrappers returned LOW risk with no upstream impacted symbols/processes; source-specific contexts were inspected for the ambiguous page symbols.
RED focused TMA tests failed for missing reporting preset helpers, missing preset chips, and Statistics not yet using shared selected period.

Focused TMA tests passed for `src/test/period.test.ts`, `src/test/period-store-and-picker.test.tsx`, `src/test/statistics-page.test.tsx`, and `src/test/period-chip-entrypoints.test.tsx` with 17 tests.

Follow-up timezone fix moved shared TMA reporting-period boundaries from UTC to Vietnam local time (`Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh` semantics via `UTC+07:00`) so month/week/custom ranges and labels line up with Vietnam day rollover instead of UTC midnight.

Focused TMA tests passed again for `src/test/period.test.ts`, `src/test/period-store-and-picker.test.tsx`, `src/test/statistics-page.test.tsx`, and `src/test/period-chip-entrypoints.test.tsx` with 18 tests after the Vietnam-time follow-up.
`./init.sh typecheck` returned `OK` after the Vietnam-time follow-up.

Period picker regression investigation found that `backTo` was incorrectly treated as proof of a local sub-page flow. Normal Home, Statistics, and household chip links therefore returned `appliedPeriod` in navigation state without updating the shared Zustand period store.

A RED regression test reproduced the real `PeriodChipLink -> /period -> BottomButton` path and confirmed the store remained on the current month. The picker now treats only callers that provide `initialPeriod` as local filter flows; normal chip entries update the shared period store before navigating back.
Focused TMA tests passed for period helpers, picker/store, chip entrypoints, and Statistics with 19 tests after the period-apply fix. `./init.sh typecheck` and `./init.sh lint` returned `OK`.

Final `./init.sh` completed with `Done!`; harness JSON, diff whitespace, and harness-size checks passed. Final GitNexus change detection reported `medium` risk across the expected PeriodPicker and BottomButton processes.
`jq empty harness/feature_index.json harness/features/feat-099.json` returned no output.

Final `gitnexus_detect_changes(scope: 'all', repo: 'household-finance-system')` reported `critical` risk with 97 changed symbols, 36 affected symbols/processes, and 10 indexed changed files. The affected processes are expected for this slice and center on the shared TMA period helper, period picker, expense filter, finance/household period flows, and Statistics analytics rendering.

notes: Worker analytics range support already exists from feat-096, so this follow-up stayed TMA-only. Real Telegram WebView visual smoke remains pending because authenticated TMA launch context is required.
